selected_raters.selection_reason: Selected before scoring from the power-zone method route (persona chose or cross-checks the valuation method).
Selected before scoring from the power-zone method route (persona chose or cross-checks the valuation method).
Selected before scoring from the power-zone method route (persona chose or cross-checks the valuation method).

explanatory_strength.rationale: The packet ties durable economics to a specific volume-to-fee croupier mechanism: FY2025 operating revenue ¥198.7B (+22.5%), cash equities ADV ¥7.52T (+31.9% YoY), ~58% operating margin, and normalized owner cash ¥70/share below peak EPS ¥76.81. Cycle peak is explicitly flagged and a downside reserve is sized. The distinguishing test is whether mid-cycle owner cash can grow 5%/4% from a normalized base; that test is stated but not yet supported by a hard-to-vary reinvestment or incremental-ROIC bridge, and the compounder framing sits awkwardly on a cyclical toll booth.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Core operating facts reconcile from scan-tier Q4 FY2025 filings (revenue, ADV, EPS, ROE 23.1%) and the four-component contract proofs are mechanically complete with overlap controls. Decision-grade gaps remain: filing_facts reports no_full_tier_text_extract; Japanese annual 20251113_j.pdf extract failed; management_facts carries zero extracted claims from 35 inventoried documents; shares outstanding and clearing pass-through split carry [HUMAN REVIEW]; the reinvestment_or_assets proof row labels owner_earnings_reinvestment_dcf but terminates at judgment values (¥160–480/sh) without reinvestment rate or incremental ROIC traces required by the quality_reinvestment route.

downside_control.rationale: Franchise downside is bounded by monopoly infrastructure, no material operating net debt, and an explicit volume-cycle reserve (base −¥180/sh on peak ADV). Permanent loss is not structurally protected at ¥2,190: contract low case implies −10.6% annualized return and −54% to low value per share; earnings remain highly volume- and rate-sensitive with ~79% payout limiting balance-sheet cushion from retained earnings.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Evident paths fail the owner-earnings reinvestment bar at spot: contract base annualized return −2.76% at ¥2,190 vs component base value ¥1,800; Lawrence filing base 1.7%; total synthesis 3.19%; bull 5.8%. Even the high contract case (3.04% annualized) sits far below a 12% quality-compounder hurdle. Capital deployment is not approvable on base economics despite franchise quality.

claims.claim: Japan Exchange Group is a durable, moated croupier within the quality_reinvestment power zone: dominant domestic exchange and clearing infrastructure with filing-supported FY2025 margins and ROE, squarely in scope for owner-earnings review.

Normalized owner cash of ¥70/share is the correct starting anchor versus peak FY2025 EPS ¥76.81, but 5%/4% growth assumptions at cycle peak are not yet proven by incremental ROIC or a filing-verified fee-versus-pass-through split.

At ¥2,190, evidenced return paths (−2.76% contract base to 5.8% bull) fail the Buffett/Weschler reinvestment hurdle; the name merits watch for a better entry on volume normalization, not approve at peak-cycle earnings.

votes.strongest_counter_explanation: Japan's structural equity re-rating and sustained retail/inbound engagement make FY2025 the new floor, not the peak: double-digit volume growth persists, clearing collateral and data monetization lift normalized owner cash above ¥70 with limited fee compression, and the market correctly pays a premium multiple for a widening-moat toll collector whose distributable cash will compound faster than the normalized base case assumes.
votes.most_important_missing_fact: Filing-verified mid-cycle normalized owner cash per share with core trading fees separated from clearing collateral pass-through, from a usable full-tier FY2025 annual securities report extract (20251113_j.pdf currently failed) plus confirmed latest diluted share count.
falsifiers: Normalized owner cash per share falls below ¥55 for two consecutive fiscal years without a disclosed structural fee cut.
Cash equities ADV declines more than 20% year over year for two consecutive quarters with no offsetting fee or data revenue.
Incremental return on capital on disclosed technology and data reinvestment stays below 12% while payout remains above 75% and share count is not reduced.
votes.specialist_findings: Within power zone (stable moat, exchanges_markets sleeve, in_power_zone true). Audited all four valuation-proof rows: core_engine owner_cash_or_dividend_discount mechanics reconcile from ¥70 normalized owner cash but apply an opaque legacy_scale factor (~1.23x) to reach ¥1,600 base without transparent economic justification; reinvestment_or_assets uses method_id owner_earnings_reinvestment_dcf yet the trace stops at judgment reinvestment_value_per_share with no reinvestment rate, incremental ROIC, or distributable-cash runway—fails route required_evidence; net_financial_claims applies a 0–60% claim_share haircut on parent equity for clearing pass-through (reasonable, wide); downside_reserve quantifies peak-cycle reserve from ADV +31.9% YoY. No comparables attached. Overlap controls pass (four unique keys, no double_counting_flags). Option scan: none valued separately. Capital requirement risk is cyclical earnings compression, not insolvency. At ¥2,190 vs ¥1,800 contract base, annualized base return −2.76%; high payout limits reinvestment optionality central to this persona.

explanatory_strength.rationale: Four quantified thesis pillars tie volume leverage (ADV ¥7.52T, +31.9% YoY), clearing collateral (+¥19.8B bridge), data monetization, and capital return to owner cash, with explicit cyclical vs structural labeling. The competitive-advantage mechanism (regulatory network monopoly, ~58.5% operating margin) is specific and filing-backed. The explanation weakens on mispricing: the dive itself states no clear signal that the market is wrong, and FY2027 guide implies flat-to-down OI versus the FY2025 peak.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: FY2025 revenue, ADV, EPS, and margin bridge are anchored to scan-tier Q4 releases with contract calculation proofs at 100% coverage. Gaps remain material for Hohn fieldwork: the Nov 2025 Japanese yuho extract failed, filing_facts_2026-07-21.json reports no_full_tier_text_extract with a stale net-income stub, shares are from FY2024 English annual, and reinvestment_or_assets (¥280/sh base) lacks incremental ROIC runway beyond +¥569M system-services growth. H1 FY2026 run-rate is absent.

downside_control.rationale: No operating net debt, stable dividend (¥61/sh), and a quantified downside_reserve component (−¥180/sh base on peak ADV) bound cyclical loss paths; contract low case implies ~−54% to ¥1,000/sh. Downside is not tight: bear scenario −6.3% p.a. and earnings remain highly levered to turnover and rate-sensitive clearing collateral at cycle peak, with limited upside/downside asymmetry at ¥2,190.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Contract base annualized return is −2.76% at ¥2,190; total synthesis 3.19%; Lawrence bull 5.8% and contract high 3.04% all sit far below the ~15% medium-term hurdle. Component base value ¥1,800/sh is ~18% below spot. Hohn return screen fails on both absolute return and upside/downside ratio at cycle-peak earnings.

claims.claim: FY2025 cash equities ADV was ¥7.52T (+31.9% YoY), driving operating revenue to ¥198.7B (+22.5%) with ~58.5% operating margin.
Normalized owner cash of ¥70/share is below spot EPS ¥76.81 to adjust for clearing pass-through and peak-volume cyclicality; contract core_engine base values this stream at ¥1,600/sh.
Universal contract sums four non-overlapping components to ¥1,800/sh base versus ¥2,190 spot (−2.76% base annualized return), with downside_reserve −¥180/sh sizing peak-cycle volume risk.

votes.strongest_counter_explanation: The market correctly prices JPX as a durable domestic exchange monopoly at cycle-peak earnings: FY2025 retail and inbound equity engagement inflated turnover and clearing collateral income, and even normalized ¥70/sh owner cash cannot compound fast enough from ¥2,190 to clear a 15% return if volume mean-reverts toward the ¥133–162B pre-FY2025 revenue band.
votes.most_important_missing_fact: Filing-verified H1 FY2026 (Apr–Sep 2025) cash-equity ADV and clearing-fee run-rate from E_ER_JPX_Q2FY2025.pdf or a successful extract of 20251113_j.pdf, to test whether the FY2025 volume spike is already normalizing before the next full-year print.
falsifiers: Two consecutive quarters with cash equities ADV below ¥5.5T without offsetting fee-rate increases
Normalized owner cash falls below ¥55/share for two fiscal years without a disclosed structural fee cut
Operating margin compresses below 50% while ADV remains above ¥6T, indicating fee-cap or mix deterioration rather than pure volume cyclicality
votes.specialist_findings: Competitive-advantage mechanics are well mapped: monopoly toll on Japan market infrastructure, volume × fee stack, clearing pass-through normalization, and peer context (CME, Deutsche Börse). Overlap controls cleanly separate core_engine, reinvestment_or_assets, net_financial_claims, and downside_reserve with no double-count flags. Audit concerns: (1) core_engine proof applies opaque legacy_scale factors (~1.23× base) between raw DCF (~¥1,300) and published component value (~¥1,600); (2) reinvestment_or_assets embeds ¥280/sh as judgment without a filing-disclosed incremental ROIC ledger; (3) normalized ¥70/sh splits core trading fees from rate-sensitive clearing collateral only at narrative level, not statutory line-item level; (4) no management fieldwork this period. Hohn power zone fit is valid (exchanges_markets, stable moat).

explanatory_strength.rationale: Filings support a hard-to-replicate domestic exchange and clearing monopoly: toll fees scale with turnover, clearing pass-through is identified, and peak FY2025 volume (+31.9% ADV YoY) is separated from structural data monetization. Inversion on volume mean-reversion and regulatory fee caps is explicit and tied to the downside_reserve component.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: Core flow metrics reconcile in scan-tier Q4 FY2025 releases, but filing_facts_2026-07-21 reports net income 30.0 (otc extract error) versus ¥79.1B in the earnings release; the Nov 2025 Japanese yuho full-tier extract failed; shares are FY2024 annual with human review flag; reinvestment_or_assets and net_financial_claims proof rows are judgment plugs, not independent DCF or NAV bridges at current scale.

downside_control.rationale: No material operating net debt and the franchise survives volume slumps, but earnings are cyclical at cycle peak and spot ~31× normalized owner cash leaves meaningful multiple-compression risk. The contract downside_reserve (−¥180/sh base) quantifies peak-cycle exposure; partial dhando, not a cheap-enough mispriced asset.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Universal contract base annualized return at ¥2,190 is −2.76% over 7 years; Lawrence filing base is 1.7% and total synthesis 3.19%, all well below a ~15% portfolio hurdle. Bull scenario 5.8% depends on sustained retail/inbound engagement that may already be in the price.

claims.claim: FY2025 operating revenue was ¥198.7B (+22.5% YoY) with cash equities ADV ¥7.52T (+31.9% YoY), confirming a volume-driven peak cycle rather than a permanent earnings baseline.
At ¥2,190 per share, contract base value ¥1,800/share implies −2.76% annualized return over 7 years, failing the portfolio return hurdle despite a stable moat.
The core_engine calculation proof applies a legacy_scale factor (~1.23×) to lift DCF output to the component schedule without a filing-sourced bridge, weakening hard-to-vary valuation proof.

votes.strongest_counter_explanation: Japan's structural equity re-rating, TSE reforms, and sustained retail/inbound engagement keep turnover and data monetization above mid-cycle, so ¥70/sh normalized owner cash is too conservative and mid-single-digit growth at ~31× normalized earnings is rational for a scarce monopoly toll booth.
votes.most_important_missing_fact: A filing-verified mid-cycle owner-cash bridge at current scale that separates core trading fees from clearing collateral pass-through, ideally from a usable full-tier yuho extract or H1 FY2026 earnings release.
falsifiers: Two consecutive quarters with cash equities ADV below ¥5.5T without a disclosed structural fee cut
Normalized owner cash per share falls below ¥55 for two consecutive fiscal years
Regulatory fee-cap legislation with quantified EPS impact exceeding the downside_reserve base case (−¥180/sh)
votes.specialist_findings: Within the Munger power zone for moat and inversion review. Competitive advantage looks stable: regulatory monopoly, network effects, and limited proprietary trading risk on core operations. Invert the lollapalooza: peak retail/inbound engagement plus clearing collateral tailwind plus ~31× normalized earnings equals a great business at the wrong point in the cycle. Component audit: overlap keys are clean and downside_reserve is appropriately negative; reinvestment_or_assets and net_financial_claims remain judgment overlays without standalone proof; legacy_scale in core_engine is opaque. Classic sit-on-your-ass franchise, wrong price at spot. Watch for volume normalization or multiple compression before approval.

pre_mortem.failure_story: We sized Japan Exchange Group at ¥2,190 as a durable domestic croupier monopoly, anchoring on normalized owner cash of ¥70 per share and a four-component contract base of ¥1,800 per share even though the packet classified the cycle at peak, contract base return was -2.76% per year, and total synthesis was only 3.19% per year—far below the ~15% accumulate hurdle. FY2025 cash equities ADV had already surged +31.9% YoY to ¥7.52T and clearing collateral income grossed up revenue through pass-through; we treated that spike as partially normalized rather than as the earnings ceiling. Post-boom mean reversion was sharper than the downside_reserve component assumed: turnover fell toward the pre-FY2025 ¥133–162B revenue band, clearing collateral income normalized with lower balances and rates, and spot EPS fell from ¥76.81 toward the contract falsifier band below ¥55 per share. The market re-rated the equity from roughly 26× peak earnings toward mid-teens on trough owner cash, so we suffered simultaneous earnings miss and multiple compression. Hold-period total return landed near the bear envelope (-6.3% per year) rather than the 1.7% Lawrence base or 3.19% synthesis case—severe underperformance on a franchise-quality position held for toll-booth durability at the wrong point in the volume cycle.
earliest_warning_signals: Payoff gate 4 failed at entry: not cheap vs normalized cash flow at peak earnings and ~26× P/E on FY2025 EPS ¥76.81.
Contract base annualized return -2.76% at ¥2,190 versus the ~15% hold hurdle; total synthesis 3.19% also failed gate 5 with no dated predictive mispricing attribute.
Cycle flagged peak: FY2025 cash equities ADV ¥7.52T (+31.9% YoY) and clearing collateral +¥19.8B YoY bridge were exceptional, not mid-cycle.
Component base value ¥1,800 per share implied paying a premium to the additive build marked decision_grade at spot ¥2,190.
FY2027 management forecast (revenue ¥205B / operating income ¥115B) already implied flat-to-down OI vs FY2025 peak ¥116.3B before any sizing.
Base growth assumptions (5% years 1–5, 4% years 6–7) conflicted with peak-cycle normalization and the flat FY2027 guide.
Deep dive proposed watch at 3.2% synthesis IRR while thesis.md still carried hold stance—human override without cycle-trough entry math.
filing_facts_2026-07-21.json returned no_full_tier_text_extract with net income 30.0 (otc_prose_regex)—automated filing QA unusable for sizing.
Japanese annual 20251113_j.pdf listed full tier but cached extract failed (garbled encoding); governance and H1 FY2026 run-rate unverified.
Milly flagged Classification IRR mismatch (returns statement 3.19% vs contract base -2.76%)—stance gate ambiguity at decision time.
valuation_route.json routed quality_reinvestment / compounder profile while classification_inputs archetype was croupier at peak cycle.
Short activist scan was batch no_hit only (short_scan_2026-05-28.md); adversarial pass flagged Tier-1 web scan as [HUMAN REVIEW], not done before sizing.
forensic_checks: Reconcile quarterly cash equities ADV and trading-fee revenue from Q4 FY2026 and Q1 FY2027 earnings releases against the volume-cycle downside_reserve (-¥180/sh base); flag if normalized owner cash falls below ¥55/sh for two consecutive years per contract falsifier.
Separate core trading fees from clearing collateral pass-through in each earnings release and explanatory materials; rebuild normalized owner cash without rate-sensitive gross-up.
OCR or re-extract 20251113_j.pdf (Nov 2025 yuho) and E_ER_JPX_Q2FY2025.pdf for filing-verified H1 FY2026 flow, share count, and related-party governance items.
Trace valuation.json and valuation_contract.json normalized_owner_cash (¥70) to FY2025 parent EPS ¥76.81 and document the clearing pass-through haircut with line-item bridge.
Compare actual FY2026/FY2027 operating income to FY2027 guide (¥115B OI) and to FY2025 peak ¥116.3B; test whether reinvestment_or_assets (+¥280/sh base) matches disclosed data/clearing/tech capex.

Sum four additive components (core_engine ¥1,600, reinvestment +¥280, net_financial_claims +¥100, downside_reserve -¥180) versus spot price and contract low/base/high returns (-10.59% / -2.76% / 3.04%).
Monitor regulatory fee-cap proposals and legislation; quantify EPS impact if exchange services fees are capped.
Confirm latest diluted shares outstanding (~1,030M from FY2024 annual) from current yuho before any reinvestment DCF refresh.
Reverse DCF at entry ¥2,190: what ADV or revenue decline restores ~15% IRR with ¥70 starting owner cash and base exit multiple 19×?
Run Tier-1 short activist registry and targeted forensic web scan; no local short_reports/ and only batch no_hit on record at packet freeze.
unresolved_items: Normalized owner cash ¥70 per share remains [Assumption] below spot EPS ¥76.81; clearing pass-through separation not verified at current scale through full-tier extract.
filing_facts_2026-07-21.json is stale/broken (no_full_tier_text_extract; net income 30.0)—automated filing QA cannot be trusted without re-extract.
Japanese annual 20251113_j.pdf extract encoding failure leaves governance, share count, and statutory detail unverified.
No filing-verified H1 FY2026 run-rate; E_ER_JPX_Q2FY2025.pdf is scan-tier only in the frozen packet.
Downside_reserve severity (-¥180/sh base) may understate ADV mean-reversion from +31.9% YoY peak; bear scenario -6.3% was sensitivity only.
Hold vs watch stance inconsistency between thesis.md and deep dive at ~3% synthesis IRR with no cycle-trough entry math executed.
Synthesis weights and qualitative -0.2pp adjustment remain human approval pending; filing-only falsifier-adjusted reference is 1.7%.
No approved third-party numeric source in base IRR; prior cross-check is context tier only.
management_facts_2026-07-21.json lists document_count 35 with zero structured claims—no primary management fieldwork on normalized clearing economics.
Shares outstanding ~1,030M from FY2024 annual flagged [HUMAN REVIEW]; dilution bridge not refreshed at decision time.
Quality_reinvestment route vs croupier peak-cycle classification not reconciled before sizing a volume-leveraged exchange at cycle high.

downside_control.rationale: No material operating net debt and the franchise survives volume slumps, but earnings are cyclical at cycle peak and spot ~26× normalized owner cash leaves meaningful multiple-compression risk. The contract downside_reserve (−¥180/sh base) quantifies peak-cycle exposure; partial dhando, not a cheap-enough mispriced asset.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Universal contract base annualized return at ¥2,190 is −2.76% over 7 years; Lawrence filing base is 1.7% and total synthesis 3.19%, all well below a ~15% portfolio hurdle. Bull scenario 5.8% depends on sustained retail/inbound engagement that may already be in the price.

claims.claim: FY2025 operating revenue was ¥198.7B (+22.5% YoY) with cash equities ADV ¥7.52T (+31.9% YoY), confirming a volume-driven peak cycle rather than a permanent earnings baseline.
At ¥2,190 per share, contract base value ¥1,800/share implies −2.76% annualized return over 7 years, failing the portfolio return hurdle despite a stable moat.
The core_engine calculation proof applies a legacy_scale factor (~1.23×) to lift DCF output to the component schedule without a filing-sourced bridge, weakening hard-to-vary valuation proof.

votes.strongest_counter_explanation: Japan's structural equity re-rating, TSE reforms, and sustained retail/inbound engagement keep turnover and data monetization above mid-cycle, so ¥70/sh normalized owner cash is too conservative and mid-single-digit growth at ~26× earnings is rational for a scarce monopoly toll booth.
votes.most_important_missing_fact: A filing-verified mid-cycle owner-cash bridge at current scale that separates core trading fees from clearing collateral pass-through, ideally from a usable full-tier yuho extract or H1 FY2026 earnings release.
falsifiers: Two consecutive quarters with cash equities ADV below ¥5.5T without a disclosed structural fee cut
Normalized owner cash per share falls below ¥55 for two consecutive fiscal years
Regulatory fee-cap legislation with quantified EPS impact exceeding the downside_reserve base case (−¥180/sh)
votes.specialist_findings: Within the Munger power zone for moat and inversion review. Competitive advantage looks stable: regulatory monopoly, network effects, and limited proprietary trading risk on core operations. Invert the lollapalooza: peak retail/inbound engagement plus clearing collateral tailwind plus ~26× normalized earnings equals a great business at the wrong point in the cycle. Component audit: overlap keys are clean and downside_reserve is appropriately negative; reinvestment_or_assets and net_financial_claims remain judgment overlays without standalone proof; legacy_scale in core_engine is opaque. Classic sit-on-your-ass franchise, wrong price at spot. Watch for volume normalization or multiple compression before approval.

rejected_averages: No incompatible valuation methods were averaged to manufacture consensus.

tests: Reconcile quarterly cash equities ADV and trading-fee revenue from Q4 FY2026 and Q1 FY2027 earnings releases against the volume-cycle downside_reserve (-¥180/sh base); flag if normalized owner cash falls below ¥55/sh for two consecutive years per contract falsifier.
Separate core trading fees from clearing collateral pass-through in each earnings release and explanatory materials; rebuild normalized owner cash without rate-sensitive gross-up.
OCR or re-extract 20251113_j.pdf (Nov 2025 yuho) and E_ER_JPX_Q2FY2025.pdf for filing-verified H1 FY2026 flow, share count, and related-party governance items.
Trace valuation.json and valuation_contract.json normalized_owner_cash (¥70) to FY2025 parent EPS ¥76.81 and document the clearing pass-through haircut with line-item bridge.
Compare actual FY2026/FY2027 operating income to FY2027 guide (¥115B OI) and to FY2025 peak ¥116.3B; test whether reinvestment_or_assets (+¥280/sh base) matches disclosed data/clearing/tech capex.

Sum four additive components (core_engine ¥1,600, reinvestment +¥280, net_financial_claims +¥100, downside_reserve -¥180) versus spot price and contract low/base/high returns (-10.59% / -2.76% / 3.04%).
Monitor regulatory fee-cap proposals and legislation; quantify EPS impact if exchange services fees are capped.
Confirm latest diluted shares outstanding (~1,030M from FY2024 annual) from current yuho before any reinvestment DCF refresh.
Reverse DCF at entry ¥2,190: what ADV or revenue decline restores ~15% IRR with ¥70 starting owner cash and base exit multiple 19×?
Run Tier-1 short activist registry and targeted forensic web scan; no local short_reports/ and only batch no_hit on record at packet freeze.
residual_risks: Normalized owner cash ¥70 per share remains [Assumption] below spot EPS ¥76.81; clearing pass-through separation not verified at current scale through full-tier extract.
filing_facts_2026-07-21.json is stale/broken (no_full_tier_text_extract; net income 30.0)—automated filing QA cannot be trusted without re-extract.
Japanese annual 20251113_j.pdf extract encoding failure leaves governance, share count, and statutory detail unverified.
No filing-verified H1 FY2026 run-rate; E_ER_JPX_Q2FY2025.pdf is scan-tier only in the frozen packet.
Downside_reserve severity (-¥180/sh base) may understate ADV mean-reversion from +31.9% YoY peak; bear scenario -6.3% was sensitivity only.
Hold vs watch stance inconsistency between thesis.md and deep dive at ~3% synthesis IRR with no cycle-trough entry math executed.
Synthesis weights and qualitative -0.2pp adjustment remain human approval pending; filing-only falsifier-adjusted reference is 1.7%.
No approved third-party numeric source in base IRR; prior cross-check is context tier only.
management_facts_2026-07-21.json lists document_count 35 with zero structured claims—no primary management fieldwork on normalized clearing economics.
Shares outstanding ~1,030M from FY2024 annual flagged [HUMAN REVIEW]; dilution bridge not refreshed at decision time.
Quality_reinvestment route vs croupier peak-cycle classification not reconciled before sizing a volume-leveraged exchange at cycle high.
adversarial_review.strongest_failure_path: We sized Japan Exchange Group at ¥2,190 as a durable domestic croupier monopoly, anchoring on normalized owner cash of ¥70 per share and a four-component contract base of ¥1,800 per share even though the packet classified the cycle at peak, contract base return was -2.76% per year, and total synthesis was only 3.19% per year—far below the ~15% accumulate hurdle. FY2025 cash equities ADV had already surged +31.9% YoY to ¥7.52T and clearing collateral income grossed up revenue through pass-through; we treated that spike as partially normalized rather than as the earnings ceiling. Post-boom mean reversion was sharper than the downside_reserve component assumed: turnover fell toward the pre-FY2025 ¥133–162B revenue band, clearing collateral income normalized with lower balances and rates, and spot EPS fell from ¥76.81 toward the contract falsifier band below ¥55 per share. The market re-rated the equity from roughly 26× peak earnings toward mid-teens on trough owner cash, so we suffered simultaneous earnings miss and multiple compression. Hold-period total return landed near the bear envelope (-6.3% per year) rather than the 1.7% Lawrence base or 3.19% synthesis case—severe underperformance on a franchise-quality position held for toll-booth durability at the wrong point in the volume cycle.

chair_synthesis.weighting_rationale: The canonical Power Zone router (8697.T/research/valuation_route.json) selected quality_reinvestment as the primary profile with score 20.0, an order of magnitude above the next-best fit (credit_and_normalized_returns and predictable_cash_flow, each 2.0). It matched on archetype=compounder, component category=operating_business, and existing methods dcf/owner_cash/reinvestment, and the router chose the method and the seated raters (buffett_weschler, hohn as primary personas; munger as cross-check) before any scoring occurred, not after seeing the vote outcome. The concrete primary method is owner_earnings_reinvestment_dcf: normalized owner cash of ¥70/share (below peak FY2025 parent EPS ¥76.81) is compounded and exit-multiplied to build the core_engine and reinvestment_or_assets components. reverse_dcf and incremental_roic_runway are corroborating checks on that same owner-cash mechanism, not separate valuation philosophies: reverse_dcf asks what volume/ADV path the ¥2,190 price already implies, and incremental_roic_runway tests whether the +¥280.07/share reinvestment_or_assets component is earned at a return above cost of capital. Both checks currently expose the same weakness the raters flagged (an opaque ~1.23x legacy_scale factor in core_engine and a judgment-only reinvestment bridge with no disclosed incremental ROIC), which is exactly why the primary method dominates: a corroborating check that surfaces a gap in the primary method's proof is evidence about the primary method's reliability, not a competing estimate to blend into it. The four-component contract value (¥1,800.4466/share base) is an additive sum of non-overlapping economic claims (core_engine, reinvestment_or_assets, net_financial_claims, downside_reserve) with model_checks.no_double_counting=true and model_checks.component_sum_reconciles=true — it is not an average of competing methods, and the committee did not average the filing-only Lawrence base (1.7%), the contract base (-2.76%), and the displayed total synthesis (3.19%) to manufacture a single consensus number. Those three figures are retained side by side below as different, clearly labeled readings of the same owner-cash economics (filing-only vs full additive contract vs qualitative-adjusted synthesis pending human approval), and the dispersion between them is preserved rather than netted out.

vote_summary.round_two: Carried forward unchanged from round one (8697.T/research/committee_work/2026-07-21/round_2/*.json). The deterministic escalation check (max score dispersion of 1 point across all four dimensions, unanimous vote, evidence_status sufficient for all three raters, expected-return midpoint spread of 2.0pp) found no trigger for a second independent round, so round two is an exact carry-forward, not a second vote.

agreed_facts.fact: 8697.T (Japan Exchange Group) is a durable, moated domestic exchange and clearing monopoly with FY2025 operating revenue ¥198.7B (+22.5% YoY), cash equities ADV ¥7.52T (+31.9% YoY), ~58.5% operating margin, and ROE 23.1%.

FY2025 was a peak-cycle year, not a permanent baseline; the +31.9% YoY ADV surge and clearing-collateral income are treated as cyclical, not structural, by all three raters and by the frozen classification (cycle=peak).

Normalized owner cash of ¥70/share, below spot FY2025 parent EPS ¥76.81, is the correct starting anchor for the primary method, though the split between core trading fees and rate-sensitive clearing collateral pass-through is not yet verified at the statutory line-item level.

At the frozen price of ¥2,190/share, the additive four-component contract base value is ¥1,800.4466/share, implying an annualized return of -2.76% over a 7-year horizon — well below any of the raters' stated ~12-15% hurdles.
agreed_facts.agreement: unanimous; identical figure cited by all three raters

agreed_facts.fact: Filing-only (Lawrence) scenario returns are bear -6.3%, base 1.7%, bull 5.8%; the displayed total-synthesis figure is 3.19%. None of these clear the stated hurdle even in the bull case.

Downside is bounded but not tight: no material operating net debt and a stable ~79%-payout dividend (¥61/share) support survival, but the contract low case is ¥1,000.30/share (-54.32% downside to low) with a -10.59% annualized return, and payout leaves limited retained-earnings cushion against a volume-cycle shock.

The core_engine calculation proof applies an unexplained ~1.23x legacy_scale factor between the raw owner-earnings DCF output (~¥1,300/share) and the published component value (~¥1,600.40/share) with no filing-sourced bridge; the reinvestment_or_assets component (+¥280.07/share) is a judgment plug without a disclosed incremental-ROIC or distributable-cash runway.
agreed_facts.agreement: unanimous; flagged independently by buffett_weschler, hohn, and munger

agreed_facts.fact: Automated filing QA is currently unusable: filing_facts_2026-07-21.json returns no_full_tier_text_extract with a stale net-income stub (30.0 vs ¥79.1B in the earnings release), and the Nov 2025 Japanese annual securities report (20251113_j.pdf) full-tier extract failed on an encoding error, so governance, share count, and H1 FY2026 run-rate remain unverified at full tier.
agreed_facts.agreement: unanimous; corroborated by the deterministic evidence tribunal and the independent pre-mortem

agreed_facts.fact: The deterministic evidence tribunal found zero disputed or unresolved material facts in the frozen packet (8697.T/research/committee_work/2026-07-21/evidence_tribunal.json: disputed_facts=[], unresolved_material_facts=[]); everything the raters disagree about below is interpretation, method fit, or documentation consistency, not a contested number.

disputed_facts.issue: Archetype/method-fit mismatch: the canonical Power Zone router classifies 8697.T as archetype=compounder and routes to quality_reinvestment (owner-earnings reinvestment DCF, seating buffett_weschler/hohn/munger), while thesis.md and valuation.json's classification_inputs independently tag the same security archetype=croupier at cycle=peak (a Stahl toll-booth/mid-cycle-normalization lens).
disputed_facts.positions: Router position: compounder framing is defensible because reinvestment_or_assets and incremental ROIC are live, priced components, not zero. Pre-mortem/Munger position: 'Quality_reinvestment route vs croupier peak-cycle classification not reconciled before sizing a volume-leveraged exchange at cycle high' — flagged as an explicit unresolved item.
disputed_facts.status: unresolved; not adjudicated by this synthesis — routed to human review before any sizing decision, since it determines which seated personas' judgment should carry the most weight

disputed_facts.issue: Internal documentation inconsistency flagged by the independent adversarial pass (Milly): the deep dive's Classification IRR field shows -2.76% while its own returns-statement / valuation-bridge line shows 3.19% for the same security in the same document.
disputed_facts.positions: No rater treats this as decision-blocking (adversarial status is complete_with_residual_risks, block_final=false), but it is an unresolved factual inconsistency in the frozen evidence, not merely a rater opinion.
disputed_facts.status: unresolved; non-blocking per Milly, flagged for correction on next deep-dive refresh

disputed_facts.issue: Score dispersion on explanatory_strength and evidence_sufficiency: munger scored explanatory_strength 4 (vs 3 for buffett_weschler and hohn) and evidence_sufficiency 2 (vs 3 for buffett_weschler and hohn), with confidence=low versus their confidence=medium.
disputed_facts.positions: Munger: the monopoly/inversion mechanism is more hard-to-vary than the other two credit, but the evidence base is thinner because filing_facts and the Japanese annual extract are both broken and reinvestment/net-financial-claims proof rows are judgment plugs, not independent DCF or NAV bridges. Buffett_weschler/hohn: mechanism is one notch less proven (compounder framing sits awkwardly on a cyclical toll booth) but scan-tier Q4 filings and the four-component proof are adequate to vote.
disputed_facts.status: unresolved but non-material — dispersion of 1 point on each dimension is below the >2-point escalation threshold (8697.T/research/committee_work/2026-07-21/manifest.json conditional_escalation.required=false), so this synthesis preserves the disagreement rather than averaging the scores into a single number

disputed_facts.issue: Silenced personas: the router excluded pabrai (asymmetry_downside) and stahl (scarce_assets/croupier) from the seated committee for this cycle, even though thesis.md's own archetype tag is 'croupier' (Stahl) and Pabrai's own dhando score in the same file is 'partial' — both directly on-point lenses for a peak-cycle volume business.
disputed_facts.positions: Router position: the exclusion followed the documented rule (method chosen before scoring, not after seeing the outcome) and quality_reinvestment scored 20.0 versus 0.0 for scarce_asset_optionality and unscored dhando/asymmetry profiles. Chair note: this is a legitimate methodological choice, not a violation, but it means no seated rater independently pressure-tested the croupier/mid-cycle-normalization framing that the classification file itself uses, or applied a dhando-style margin-of-safety screen distinct from the reinvestment lens.
disputed_facts.status: unresolved; flagged for human consideration of whether a stahl or pabrai cross-check should be added on the next refresh, not for this cycle's vote

preserved_dissent.note: All three independent raters voted watch with no vote split, so there is no minority vote to preserve. The dissent that matters here is the shared bull counter-explanation each rater raised against their own base case — it is preserved verbatim below rather than netted into the base return figures.

bull_counter_explanation_by_rater.counter_explanation: Japan's structural equity re-rating and sustained retail/inbound engagement make FY2025 the new floor, not the peak: double-digit volume growth persists, clearing collateral and data monetization lift normalized owner cash above ¥70 with limited fee compression, and the market correctly pays a premium multiple for a widening-moat toll collector whose distributable cash will compound faster than the normalized base case assumes.

The market correctly prices JPX as a durable domestic exchange monopoly at cycle-peak earnings: FY2025 retail and inbound equity engagement inflated turnover and clearing collateral income, and even normalized ¥70/sh owner cash cannot compound fast enough from ¥2,190 to clear a 15% return if volume mean-reverts toward the ¥133-162B pre-FY2025 revenue band.

Japan's structural equity re-rating, TSE reforms, and sustained retail/inbound engagement keep turnover and data monetization above mid-cycle, so ¥70/sh normalized owner cash is too conservative and mid-single-digit growth at ~26x earnings is rational for a scarce monopoly toll booth.

preserved_dissent.pre_mortem_failure_path_preserved_verbat: We sized Japan Exchange Group at ¥2,190 as a durable croupier monopoly, trusting normalized owner cash of ¥70 per share and a four-component contract base of ¥1,800 per share despite cycle classification at peak and contract base return of -2.76% per year (total synthesis 3.19%) far below the ~15% accumulate hurdle. FY2025 was treated as partially normalized when cash equities ADV had already surged +31.9% YoY to ¥7.52T and clearing collateral income inflated the revenue bridge. The post-boom mean reversion was sharper than the downside_reserve component assumed. Hold-period total return landed near the bear envelope (-6.3% per year) rather than the 1.7% Lawrence base or 3.19% synthesis case — severe underperformance on a franchise-quality position held for toll-booth durability at the wrong point in the volume cycle.

value_and_entry_ranges.price_vs_base_value: Spot is approximately 21.7% above the additive contract base value (2190 / 1800.4466 - 1), i.e. the packet's own margin-of-safety anchor, not just a bearish rater opinion.
value_and_entry_ranges.entry_consideration_zone: No price computed in the frozen packet clears the raters' stated ~12-15% hurdle; the contract's own base value (¥1,800.4466/share, about 18% below spot) is the first margin-of-safety checkpoint the primary method would need before re-approaching an approve vote, and the low case (¥1,000.30/share) is the level at which the packet's own bounded low estimate would begin to look like a portfolio-grade return. A specific reverse-DCF entry price (what ADV/revenue path at ¥2,190 the market is already paying for, and what price would earn ~15% from ¥70 starting owner cash at a 19x base exit multiple) is listed as an open forensic check in the pre-mortem and has not been computed by this synthesis; the chair is not issuing a price target or sizing instruction.
value_and_entry_ranges.caveat: This is a value-range description drawn from the frozen packet, not a chair-issued price target, entry order, or capital/sizing decision.

chair_synthesis.recommendation: Watch, do not initiate or add to a position at the current ¥2,190 price. All three independently seated raters (buffett_weschler, hohn, munger), across three distinct independence groups, converged on 'watch' with sufficient evidence and no material score dispersion (all four score dimensions within a 1-point range, expected-return midpoints within 2.0pp of each other) — the deterministic escalation check found no trigger for a second independent round, so this is not an averaged or diluted consensus but three separately-reasoned confirmations of the same conclusion. The primary quality_reinvestment method (owner-earnings reinvestment DCF) and its two corroborating checks (reverse_dcf, incremental_roic_runway) all point the same way: at ¥2,190 the contract base value of ¥1,800.4466/share implies -2.76% per year over 7 years, the filing-only base is 1.7%, and even the bull case (5.8%) and contract high case (3.04%) sit far below any of the stated ~12-15% hurdles. The franchise quality, moat, and downside bounding (no material operating net debt, stable dividend) are not disputed — the reason to hold off is price relative to a peak-cycle owner-cash base, not business quality. Before any capital decision, the human owner should resolve the unreconciled compounder-vs-croupier archetype conflict (which determines whether reinvestment or mid-cycle-normalization framing should dominate on the next cycle), obtain a usable full-tier extract of the Japanese annual securities report and a filing-verified H1 FY2026 run-rate (both currently broken), and treat the shared bull counter-explanation above as the live falsifier to monitor rather than a reason to override three independent 'watch' votes today. This synthesis makes no capital allocation or position-sizing decision; that remains with human_decision.json.

evidence_refresh_dates: Next quarterly earnings release (Q1 FY2027) for updated ADV, revenue, and clearing-collateral bridge.
Next annual securities report (yuho) filing following FY2026 fiscal year-end (2027-03-31).
valuation_refresh_triggers: Normalized owner cash per share falls below ¥55 for two consecutive fiscal years without a disclosed structural fee cut (shared falsifier, all three raters).
Two consecutive quarters with cash equities ADV below ¥5.5T without a disclosed structural fee cut.
Cash equities ADV declines more than 20% year over year for two consecutive quarters with no offsetting fee or data revenue.
Operating margin compresses below 50% while ADV remains above ¥6T, indicating fee-cap or mix deterioration rather than pure volume cyclicality.
Regulatory fee-cap legislation or proposal with a quantified EPS impact.
Incremental return on capital on disclosed technology/data reinvestment stays below 12% while payout remains above 75% and share count is not reduced.
price_review_thresholds: Price approaches or falls to the additive contract base value of approximately ¥1,800/share (the primary method's own margin-of-safety anchor).
Price falls toward the contract low-case value of approximately ¥1,000/share, where the packet's own bounded low estimate would begin to imply a portfolio-grade return.
Price rises further while normalized owner cash and ADV show early signs of mean reversion, widening the gap between spot and the base contract value rather than closing it.
thesis_break_conditions: Franchise moat impairment: loss of exclusive domestic clearing/exchange infrastructure position or material competitive entry.
Operating net debt becomes material, removing the current downside bound cited by all three raters.

The compounder-vs-croupier archetype conflict resolves toward croupier/mid-cycle-normalization framing with no credible reinvestment runway, which would remove the basis for the currently seated quality_reinvestment primary method itself.
Two consecutive years of normalized owner cash below ¥55/share (shared hard falsifier).
expected_catalyst_dates: Next earnings release carrying an update to the FY2027 management guide (revenue ¥205B / operating income ¥115B) versus the FY2025 peak (¥116.3B operating income).
Any TSE/JPX regulatory fee-cap proposal or legislative announcement.

synthesis.strongest_dissent: Japan's structural equity re-rating and sustained retail/inbound engagement make FY2025 the new floor, not the peak: double-digit volume growth persists, clearing collateral and data monetization lift normalized owner cash above ¥70 with limited fee compression, and the market correctly pays a premium multiple for a widening-moat toll collector whose distributable cash will compound faster than the normalized base case assumes.
unresolved_items: A filing-verified mid-cycle owner-cash bridge at current scale that separates core trading fees from clearing collateral pass-through, ideally from a usable full-tier yuho extract or H1 FY2026 earnings release.

Filing-verified H1 FY2026 (Apr–Sep 2025) cash-equity ADV and clearing-fee run-rate from E_ER_JPX_Q2FY2025.pdf or a successful extract of 20251113_j.pdf, to test whether the FY2025 volume spike is already normalizing before the next full-year print.

Filing-verified mid-cycle normalized owner cash per share with core trading fees separated from clearing collateral pass-through, from a usable full-tier FY2025 annual securities report extract (20251113_j.pdf currently failed) plus confirmed latest diluted share count.

dissent_ledger.issue: A filing-verified mid-cycle owner-cash bridge at current scale that separates core trading fees from clearing collateral pass-through, ideally from a usable full-tier yuho extract or H1 FY2026 earnings release.

Filing-verified H1 FY2026 (Apr–Sep 2025) cash-equity ADV and clearing-fee run-rate from E_ER_JPX_Q2FY2025.pdf or a successful extract of 20251113_j.pdf, to test whether the FY2025 volume spike is already normalizing before the next full-year print.

component_review.decision_rule: Each assigned rater records accept, challenge, or reject with a proposed range and evidence. The aggregator may not silently average disputed ranges.

mandatory_checks: Is the valuation method appropriate for this economic claim?
Is the security's ownership claim and economic-unit denominator reconciled correctly?
Does the evidence support the low/base/high range and its timing?
Are comparable assets truly like-for-like after production, contract, location, capital, ownership, and realization adjustments?
For an option, are success probability, time to realization, and remaining owner-funded capital explicit?
Is the component additive, or already embedded in another component?
What observation would move the base estimate materially?

Regulatory fee-cap legislation or proposal with a quantified EPS impact.
Incremental return on capital on disclosed technology/data reinvestment stays below 12% while payout remains above 75% and share count is not reduced.
price_review_thresholds: Price approaches or falls to the additive contract base value of approximately ¥1,800/share (the primary method's own margin-of-safety anchor).
Price falls toward the contract low-case value of approximately ¥1,000/share, where the packet's own bounded low estimate would begin to imply a portfolio-grade return.
Price rises further while normalized owner cash and ADV show early signs of mean reversion, widening the gap between spot and the base contract value rather than closing it.
thesis_break_conditions: Franchise moat impairment: loss of exclusive domestic clearing/exchange infrastructure position or material competitive entry.
Operating net debt becomes material, removing the current downside bound cited by all three raters.

The compounder-vs-croupier archetype conflict resolves toward croupier/mid-cycle-normalization framing with no credible reinvestment runway, which would remove the basis for the currently seated quality_reinvestment primary method itself.
Two consecutive years of normalized owner cash below ¥55/share (shared hard falsifier).
expected_catalyst_dates: Next earnings release carrying an update to the FY2027 management guide (revenue ¥205B / operating income ¥115B) versus the FY2025 peak (¥116.3B operating income).
Any TSE/JPX regulatory fee-cap proposal or legislative announcement.

provenance.model: five-call baseline with conditional escalation and deterministic support assembly
